title: ZREMRANGEBYRANK
description: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of indexes. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.
complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements removed by the operation.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Removes all elements in the sorted set stored at `key` with rank between `start` and `stop`. Both `start` and `stop` are `0` -based indexes with `0` being the element with the lowest score. These indexes can be negative numbers, where they indicate offsets starting at the element with the highest score. For example: `-1` is the element with the highest score, `-2` the element with the second highest score and so forth. ## Examples ZADD myzset 1 "one" ZADD myzset 2 "two" ZADD myzset 3 "three" ZREMRANGEBYRANK myzset 0 1 ZRANGE myzset 0 -1 WITHSCORES ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of members removed. **RESP3:** [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): Number of members removed.
intro:
Removes all elements in the sorted set stored at `key` with rank between `start`
and `stop`.
Both `start` and `stop` are `0` -based indexes with `0` being the element with
the lowest score.
These indexes can be negative numbers, where they indicate offsets starting at
the element with the highest score.
For example: `-1` is the element with the highest score, `-2` the element with
the second highest score and so forth.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Removes all elements in the sorted set stored at `key` with rank between `start`
and `stop`.
Both `start` and `stop` are `0` -based indexes with `0` being the element with
the lowest score.
These indexes can be negative numbers, where they indicate offsets starting at
the element with the highest score.
For example: `-1` is the element with the highest score, `-2` the element with
the second highest score and so forth.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
ZADD myzset 1 "one"
ZADD myzset 2 "two"
ZADD myzset 3 "three"
ZREMRANGEBYRANK myzset 0 1
ZRANGE myzset 0 -1 WITHSCORES

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of members removed.

**RESP3:**

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): Number of members removed.
